title: Panopticon User Context
description:
The shape of the user-provided context document. Stored as a single versioned JSON document, NOT normalized into rows — this lets the user edit holistically and keeps semantics together. Phase 2 populates this via the intake flow; Phase 1 just defines the schema and provides a placeholder UI.

IMPORTANT: This schema is committed to the public repository. The FILLED-IN version of this document is gitignored under `personal/` and lives only on the user's device. Never commit a populated context document.

schema_version.description: Schema version of this context document. Bumped on breaking shape changes.

last_updated_utc.description: Epoch milliseconds of the most recent edit.

demographics.description: Optional demographic data. Used as context for framing, NEVER as a predictor or scoring input. See ETHICS.md hard rail #1.

background.description: Background and life history. The coaching layer uses this to *frame* current behavior (be gentler when context warrants), never to predict outcomes.

childhood_stability.description: Free-text or enum description of childhood stability. Optional.

diagnoses.description: Diagnoses the user has and chooses to share with the coaching layer. Trigger trauma-informed framing.

stated_values.description: What the user says they value. The coaching layer surfaces gaps between stated values and observed behavior.

known_patterns.description: Patterns the user has already self-identified. The system may confirm, refine, or contradict these as data accumulates.

preferred_coach_persona.description: Selected persona for the coaching layer. See prompts/personas/.
